rows.notes: Centered page title. Handwriting of heading is legible though last word is archaic/Latin; I read it as 'Sarniensi' (parish name) based on letter shapes and context at top of page.
Left margin date. Clear numeric year and month; no amounts present.

rows.description: W. Taylors Time for L. Tew --
rows.notes: Description and monetary columns well aligned with vertical rules. Pounds '02', shillings '18', pence '4' are clear and within valid ranges.
Amounts aligned in columns: '01' pounds, '13' shillings, '0' pence are clearly written and unambiguous.

rows.description: Mr. Monsonthorne's fine is not charged to Parish
rows.notes: This interline remark is faint and partially abbreviated; appears to be an explanatory note about a fine not being charged. No amounts are aligned with the monetary columns for this row. The surname spelling is uncertain (read as 'Monsonthorne' tentatively).
Amounts read as '02' pounds, '10' shillings, '0' pence. Vertical alignment with other rows is clear; handwriting of name and purpose is legible.
Amounts read as '02' / '01' / '8'. Surname/place uncertain (appears as 'Binnett' and 'Rensington'); letters are slightly cramped but numeric columns are legible.
Left margin date marking a new section. Clear year and day-month; no amounts associated.
Centered section heading. Legible Latin phrase indicating miscellaneous payments.
Pounds '08' and shillings '5' are clear. Pence looked like '4' with a small mark adjacent; interpreted as whole pence 4. There is some smudging near the pence column so fraction is left blank.

Description is abbreviated and a few words uncertain (read as 'Brewers & Const. (Tyers?) & Guard'); numeric columns read '02' / '13' / '8' fairly clearly, but the description uncertainty lowers confidence.

rows.description: To Thos. Todd his ??? of superannuation --
rows.notes: Surname 'Todd' clear; intervening words are cramped/abbreviated. Monetary columns read as '05 0 0' aligned under the vertical rules, but the text line has moderate ambiguity.

rows.description: Subscr. to Septuag. Vers. of J. T. by order --
rows.notes: Appears to be subscription payment. Columns read as '05' / '05' / '0' clearly; abbreviated words (Septuag. vers.) interpreted from Latin/ledger context.
Description reads 'oil & lamplighting' clearly. Amounts '04' / '09' / '3' are well aligned and legible.

rows.description: To Robt. Chandler his Bill in full --
rows.notes: Surname 'Chandler' legible. Monetary column is somewhat smudged; there appears to be a '12' in pounds and '1' in shillings but the pence looked like '12' in the scan (which would be invalid). I have interpreted that mark conservatively as pence '2' to keep pence within valid range; this is a low-confidence normalization.
Description uncertain in one surname ('Battery'/'Bathery'); monetary columns read clearly as '00' / '15' / '6'.
Description abbreviated ('Garden y'th' / 'sundries'); amounts '00' / '15' / '0' are properly aligned and legible.
Payment for book-binding; numeric columns read as '00' / '05' / '0' clearly. The first word of description ('Robury') is faint but inferred from context.
Multiple trades listed; 'Teasler' and 'Slater' partly uncertain but readable. Amounts appear as '5' / '14' / '3' though the hundreds column and pence strokes are slightly blurred.
Short description and small amounts '00' / '02' / '6' are clear and well aligned.

rows.description: Pavier, Smith, in Reeve of Shed --
rows.notes: Description words are abbreviated and uncertain ('Pavier/Parer/Player'), but amounts '02' / '02' / '3' sit clearly in the three monetary columns.
Name and occupation legible. Monetary columns read '16' / '18' / '3' clearly; shillings 18 within valid range.
Short descriptive date and items (wine); amounts '01' / '07' / '6' are clearly written.

rows.description: Colln. for a poor Door by order --
rows.notes: Abbreviation 'Colln.' (collection) is clear. Amounts appear as '05' / '05' / '0' aligned to the rules.

rows.description: Mr. Fletcher for 3 Com. Prayers etc. --
rows.notes: Clerical payment for prayers; 'Mr Fletcher' clear. Monetary columns read '01' / '13' / '9' though the abbreviations in the description slightly reduce confidence.

Description likely for 'cooks' on Nov 2; '19' shillings is legible but unusually high for a small item, hence moderate confidence in interpretation of the description though numeric columns are readable.
Phrase 'The same' references previous line(s). Numeric columns read '03' / '19' / '6' clearly, description context inferred from previous rows.
Again 'The same' phrase. Amounts '10' / '09' / '9' are legible. Description abbreviation 'Janry.' is clear enough.
Short line; numeric columns '01' / '03' / '1' are clear and unambiguous.
Name 'Bolton Chamber' readable; the rightmost pence figure is small and slightly smudged — interpreted as '2' pence to keep within valid range.
Description and numerals are clear: '01' / '10' / '4'. 'Gaudies' (festive entertainments) inferred from context.
Short housekeeping expense; numeric columns read '00' / '05' / '0' with good clarity.
Description clear; amounts '00' / '19' / '7' legible. Shillings at maximum typical range but valid.
Item and date clear; amounts '00' / '15' / '4' are distinct and aligned.

The pence area contains a small flourish after the digit that resembles a halfpenny symbol ('ob'). I read the numerals as '00' / '03' / '2' with a halfpenny, hence pence_fraction 0.5. The flourish is faint so confidence is moderate.

Large payment: '33' pounds and '15' shillings are clear. The pence column shows '3' with a trailing small loop/mark interpreted as three-farthings (0.75). The fraction is faint but consistent with a '3q' style mark in this manuscript.
Simple line item; amounts '00' / '12' / '0' aligned and clear.
Monetary columns '26' / '11' / '9' are distinct and unambiguous; 'Law Charges' reads clearly.
Small payment for a cow; numeric columns '00' / '01' / '0' are clear though the first word of description ('Landmans') is slightly worn.

Bottom right running total. Pounds '234' and shillings '8' are legible. Pence read as '10' with a small fractional mark resembling a farthing ('1/4' or 0.25) — interpreted as 10 pence and a quarter (0.25). Mark is faint but fits typical ledger notation for 1/4.
